findings.summary: The worker loop and repository scheduler adapter call leases.release() in a finally block without catching exceptions, causing the worker daemon process to crash with an unhandled LeaseOwnershipLostError when a run is cancelled or a lease is lost.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap leases.release({ ... }) in a try-catch block inside the finally blocks of both runClaimedJob (worker-loop.ts) and defaultWorkerLoop (repository-scheduler-adapter.ts) to safely ignore or log expected LeaseOwnershipLostErrors.

findings.summary: ResumeRun calls leases.release() in its catch block without a nested try-catch, which will mask the original error with a LeaseOwnershipLostError if the lease was lost.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap leases.release() in a try-catch block to prevent it from throwing and masking the original error.
